content.problemStatement:
You are given two strings SSS and TTT. 

Find the length of the largest string KKK such that is is a subsequence of both SSS and TTT.


Input Format

First line of the input contains the string SSS.
Second line of the input contains the string TTT.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum size of the subsequence that is common in SSS and TTT.
constraints: SSS and TTT are made up of lower case english characters.

code.Python3:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    s = input()
    t = input()

    n = len(s)
    m = len(t)

    dp = [[0] * (m + 1) for _ in range(n + 1)]

    for i in range(n + 1):
        # Base cases
        dp[i][0] = 0
    for j in range(m + 1):
        # Base cases
        dp[0][j] = 0

    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        for j in range(1, m + 1):
            # DP transitions
            dp[i][j] = max(dp[i - 1][j], dp[i][j - 1], (s[i - 1] == t[j - 1]) + dp[i - 1][j - 1])

    print(dp[n][m])
